summary: First implementation testing completed. All backend APIs and frontend features working correctly. BLE scanning and connection status are properly mocked.
critical_code_review_comments: All React Native components properly used (View, Text, TouchableOpacity)
All text wrapped in Text components
testID attributes present on all interactive elements
BLE mocking clearly implemented and documented
seed_data_creation: Test devices created and cleaned up during backend tests. Frontend testing created Machine and Pod devices via UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features working correctly. Backend API handles device CRUD, role reassignment, and duplicate MAC updates. Frontend properly implements BLE mocking, device registration, bilingual support (EN/ES), and connection status indicators. No issues found.

backend_tests.devices_crud: ✓ GET /api/devices returns array, POST creates devices with persistence, DELETE removes devices, PUT updates roles
backend_tests.duplicate_handling: ✓ POST with same MAC updates instead of creating duplicate
backend_tests.role_assignment: ✓ Assigning role to device B unassigns from device A (both POST and PUT)

frontend_tests.controls_tab: ✓ Logo displayed, no machine warning shows/hides correctly, pods mode selector (disabled/sequential/random), time interval +/- buttons (0.1-10s range), speed +/- buttons (0-10 range), Speed/Launch/Init buttons visible and tappable
frontend_tests.devices_tab: ✓ SCAN FOR DEVICES button, mock BLE scanning (2.5s delay, 4 devices discovered: JDY-32, JDY-23 variants), device registration with role assignment (Machine/Pod1/Pod2/Pod3), registered devices show in list with connection status
frontend_tests.settings_tab: ✓ Vibrator/Pods/Heater toggles visible and functional, Language switcher EN/ES works, all text translates correctly
frontend_tests.navigation: ✓ Tab navigation works between Controls, Devices, Settings
frontend_tests.connection_status: ✓ Header shows connection indicators (M, P1, P2, P3) with colored dots (green=connected, yellow=connecting, red=disconnected)
frontend_tests.device_removal: ✓ Remove button (red X) deletes devices, connection status updates accordingly
frontend_tests.pods_mode_logic: ✓ Disabled with 0 pods, Sequential with 1 pod, Sequential/Random options with 2+ pods
